text: Handling deadlines can be stressful, but good time management makes a big difference. Start by breaking your project into smaller tasks and setting mini-deadlines for each. Prioritization is key: focus on what is most important or urgent first. If the workload feels too large, consider scope management—talk to your team or manager about adjusting the project’s expectations. Finally, remember that communication is essential. If you realize you might miss a deadline, inform the relevant people early. This allows everyone to find a solution together. With these strategies, meeting deadlines becomes much more manageable and less overwhelming.
